intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant Esula virgata. However, I found information on the plant Echium plantagineum, which is also known as Paterson's curse or Echium. 

If we assume that you are referring to Echium plantagineum, here's a paragraph about the plant:

Echium plantagineum is a flowering plant that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae). The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, skin irritations, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Echium plantagineum include echinacoside, isohinacin, and echium oil, which have been found to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The most common herbal preparations made from Echium plantagineum include teas, tinctures, and topical creams, although its use is not well established in modern medicine.
